title: Chord Inversions
text: Chord InversionsWe've talked a bit about chord inversions, so let's now look at them in more detail.Here are the inversions of the C major triad:And the inversions of the A minor triad:And the inversions of a dominant seventh chord:Other types of seventh chords can also be inverted three times. Here is C minor 7:Try it for yourself! Practise inverting triads and seventh chords on the keyboard, and also write them down.Using the Root Position for a Final ChordIt is common to end on a root position chord, for the sake of finality and stability that comes with this.This is 'Twinkle Twinkle Little Star' finishing on the root position tonic triad:(played at approximately 04:15 in the 'Chord Inversions' video)Chord progressions Ic - V7 - I in F Major Passing second inversionAt 5:50 in the video, John played this example where the second inversion is again used. We are still in F major:
